$comment: Deny-by-default dependency-license allowlist (design lock L-57 / O-7). 'allowed_licenses' are SPDX identifiers acceptable for code linked into shipped engine cores. 'dependency_licenses' maps each declared dependency name to its verified SPDX license; a dependency missing from this map counts as UNKNOWN and fails CI. 'v8' is V8 (BSD-3-Clause), now a SHIPPED runtime dependency of the in-process JS host (src/runtime/js/, issue #76 / L-61): acquired outside vcpkg via the SHA-pinned denoland/rusty_v8 prebuilt channel (tools/v8-prebuilt.json + tools/fetch_v8.py; the wgpu-native/CEF third-party-prebuilt precedent in docs/signing.md — TLS + SHA-pin + verify-before-use, out of scope for the first-party R-SEC-009 trust root). The rusty_v8 static archive bundles V8 (BSD-3-Clause) + Chromium libc++ (Apache-2.0 WITH LLVM-exception, allowlisted) statically; 'rusty_v8' is the MIT-licensed Rust binding project whose extern-"C" shim layer (v8__*) the hybrid host consumes for the two STL-crossing ops. (The earlier v8-v143-x64 13.0.245.25 NuGet monolith was the js-engine M0 SPIKE's throwaway stand-in — see the recorded L-42 deviation in spikes/js-engine/FINDINGS.md.) A from-source owned vcpkg V8 port is a separate pre-1.0 hardening task (src/runtime/js/README.md § From-source migration seam). 'cef' is CEF (the Chromium Embedded Framework), the M5 editor-GUI runtime (L-15 / L-41 / R-UI-007): pinned binary distribution 149.0.6+g0d0eeb6+chromium-149.0.7827.201 from the Spotify CDN. Originally the cef-compositing M0 spike's throwaway pin, PROMOTED by M5-F0a (issue #150) to the production build substrate — the SAME pinned build is now fetched + SHA-256-verified-before-use via tools/fetch_cef.py + tools/cef-prebuilt.json behind the CONTEXT_BUILD_GUI_CEF option (src/editor/cef/), the same outside-vcpkg signed-prebuilt treatment as wgpu-native/rusty_v8 (out of scope for the first-party R-SEC-009 trust root, not via tools/verify_artifact.py). CEF itself is BSD-3-Clause and the distribution bundles Chromium/third-party notices in its LICENSE.txt / CREDITS.html (a shipped-editor CEF integration still needs a fuller third-party notice pass — an M5-F0b/packaging concern, not this substrate task). 'wgpu-native' is the webgpu M0 spike's prebuilt GitHub-release binary (v29.0.1.1) — likewise spike-only, never shipped, acquired outside vcpkg (no vcpkg port exists); dual-licensed 'MIT OR Apache-2.0', we elect Apache-2.0; its bundled webgpu.h/wgpu.h headers are BSD-3-Clause (webgpu-headers, also allowlisted); the recorded L-42 deviation lives in spikes/webgpu/FINDINGS.md. 'wasmtime' (prebuilt C API 46.0.1) and 'wasm-micro-runtime' (WAMR 2.4.5, built from a pinned source tarball; its 'wamrc' AOT compiler prebuilt is the same project/license) are the wasm M0 spike's runtimes — likewise spike-only, acquired outside vcpkg, recorded for clearance; deviations in spikes/wasm/FINDINGS.md. 'esbuild' (MIT) is the M3 TypeScript toolchain's transpiler/bundler (src/runtime/ts/, issue #83 / R-LANG-002): a build-TIME native binary acquired outside vcpkg via the SHA-pinned esbuild-own-npm-package channel (tools/ts-toolchain.json + tools/fetch_esbuild.py; the SAME wgpu-native/CEF/rusty_v8 third-party-prebuilt precedent — TLS + SHA-pin + verify-before-use, out of scope for the first-party R-SEC-009 trust root). Unlike rusty_v8, esbuild is NEVER linked into the shipped engine (it is invoked as a subprocess), so this is a build-tool clearance entry, recorded for provenance the same way the spike runtimes are. 'tsgo' (Apache-2.0) is the M3 TS SEMANTIC typechecker (microsoft/typescript-go) added by the #85 author->typecheck->fix loop (src/runtime/ts/ts_typecheck.h): the SAME build-tool treatment as esbuild — a SHA-pinned native binary acquired outside vcpkg via the TypeScript team's OWN @typescript/native-preview per-platform npm packages (tools/tsc-toolchain.json + tools/fetch_tsc.py; TLS + SHA-pin + verify-before-use, out of scope for the first-party R-SEC-009 trust root), invoked as a subprocess (--noEmit) and NEVER linked into the shipped engine (esbuild deliberately does NOT typecheck, it strips types); recorded here for provenance the same way esbuild is. 'glslang', 'spirv-cross' and 'spirv-tools' are the native shader cross-compile toolchain added behind the opt-in CONTEXT_BUILD_SHADER_CROSSCOMPILE path (R-REND-005, issue #125, sub-task B of #119) via the sanctioned vcpkg channel (VCPKG_MANIFEST_FEATURES=shader-crosscompile); like the wgpu backend the path is CI-gated and never resolved by the default build. 'tint' and 'naga' are that same flag-gated path's WGSL leg (issue #133, sub-task D of #119 — measured ruling in docs/wgsl-tool-decision.md): both are standalone CLIs invoked as SUBPROCESSES, never linked into the engine (the esbuild build-tool precedent). 'tint' (google/dawn — the WIRED SPIR-V->WGSL tool) is BSD-3-Clause, built FROM SOURCE at the Dawn tag pinned in tools/tint-toolchain.json (publisher TLS + pinned-commit verify-before-build, fail-closed; no official standalone prebuilts exist). 'naga' (gfx-rs/wgpu's naga-cli — the roundtrip test's native-consumer cross-validator + the evaluation-harness alternative) is dual-licensed 'MIT OR Apache-2.0' — we elect Apache-2.0, matching the wgpu-native election; installed from source via cargo (tools/naga-toolchain.json + tools/fetch_naga.py; crates.io checksums enforced by cargo itself). 'spirv-cross' (KhronosGroup/SPIRV-Cross) and 'spirv-tools' (KhronosGroup/SPIRV-Tools) are Apache-2.0. 'glslang' (KhronosGroup/glslang) is the multi-licensed SPDX expression 'Apache-2.0 AND BSD-3-Clause AND MIT AND GPL-3.0-or-later' (per the microsoft/vcpkg port manifest); every conjunct EXCEPT the GPL-3.0-or-later is already allowlisted (Apache-2.0/BSD-3-Clause/MIT). The GPL-3.0-or-later conjunct is SOLELY the preprocessor's Bison-generated parser skeleton, which carries the GNU Bison runtime 'special exception' ('you may create a larger work that contains part or all of the Bison parser skeleton and distribute that work under terms of your choice, so long as that work isn't itself a parser generator') — it therefore imposes NO copyleft on the linked engine, and the license the shipped/linked glslang runtime is governed by is permissive. We record 'Apache-2.0' as glslang's governing SPDX id (the deny-by-default map stores one id per dep, and all of glslang's non-exception conjuncts are allowlisted); this mirrors the Vulkan-SDK/industry precedent of shipping glslang under its permissive terms. 'miniaudio' is the M6 P6 audio backbone (src/packages/audio/, issue #184 / R-SYS-006 / L-46): a single-header C audio library VENDORED into the repo at src/packages/audio/third_party/miniaudio/miniaudio.h (pinned v0.11.22), compiled into context_audio as its own warnings-suppressed implementation TU with only the NULL device backend enabled (MA_ENABLE_ONLY_SPECIFIC_BACKENDS + MA_ENABLE_NULL — no hardware backends linked). Like the wgpu-native/CEF/rusty_v8 third-party sources it is acquired OUTSIDE vcpkg (a committed single header, not a vcpkg port), so this is a recorded-for-provenance entry the deny-by-default scanner does not itself see; miniaudio is dual-licensed 'Unlicense OR MIT-0' (public-domain dedication OR MIT No Attribution) — we elect 'MIT-0' (a valid SPDX id whose only obligation over MIT is that it waives attribution), newly allowlisted. Notes: blake3 is dual-licensed 'CC0-1.0 OR Apache-2.0' — we elect Apache-2.0; 'Apache-2.0 WITH LLVM-exception' is a valid SPDX expression whose exception only ADDS permissions relative to plain Apache-2.0 (waives Section-4 notice duties for compiled-binary redistribution), so allowlisting it is strictly no-riskier than Apache-2.0 itself. 'freetype' is the M7 runtime-UI text rasterizer (src/packages/ui/text/, issue #237 / R-UI-005): FreeType 2.13.3 built FROM the SHA-pinned official source tarball (tools/freetype-source.json -> cmake/ContextFreetype.cmake fetches + verifies + add_subdirectory's FreeType's own first-class CMake — the WAMR-from-pinned-source precedent, NOT an opaque prebuilt: portable C that compiles under EVERY toolchain incl. the local Strawberry-GCC dev gate, so unlike V8/wgpu-native/CEF/wasmtime it is a real local build). FreeType is dual-licensed 'FTL OR GPL-2.0-or-later' (third_party/freetype/FTL.TXT vs GPLv2.TXT); we ELECT the FreeType License (FTL) and NEVER the GPL-2.0-or-later limb (owner-delegated Fable decision-agent verdict = GO, .claude/plans/designs/2026-07-13-m7-runtime-ui/DECISION-a7a8-deps.md). FTL (newly added to allowed_licenses) is a permissive BSD-with-advertising-style license whose only non-trivial obligation is a product-documentation CREDIT ('This software is based in part on the work of the FreeType Team') that flows down to every shipped Product embedding the text stack — documented in src/packages/ui/text/README.md. The build DISABLES every optional subsystem (FT_DISABLE_ZLIB/BZIP2/PNG/HARFBUZZ/BROTLI), so NO transitive zlib/libpng/harfbuzz/brotli/bzip2 license enters the shipped engine and the FreeType<->HarfBuzz circular option is severed (R-SEC-006). Upstream LICENSE.TXT/FTL.TXT/GPLv2.TXT are vendored verbatim under third_party/freetype/ (NOTICE flow-down). EMBEDDED FONTS (miniaudio-style provenance rows — the fonts are DATA vendored under third_party/fonts/, not linked code, so the deny-by-default code-license scanner does not itself see them AND their OFL-1.1 is deliberately NOT added to allowed_licenses, which governs CODE linked into the engine core): (1) 'Noto Sans Regular' version 2.015 (release NotoSans-v2.015), source github.com/notofonts/latin-greek-cyrillic, SIL Open Font License 1.1 (OFL-1.1), vendored as the FULL unmodified upstream Regular face at third_party/fonts/NotoSans-Regular.ttf (sha256 f3961a9cde016d41a4879aecda1474d3a36d6bf54fa0e4643de029cc2248b0e8) beside its upstream OFL.txt + copyright ('Copyright 2022 The Noto Project Authors'); verified NO Reserved Font Name (name table IDs 0/13 carry no 'Reserved Font Name' clause -> redistribution + subsetting permitted); NOT a subset, so NOT a Modified Version. (2) 'Noto Sans Arabic Regular' version 2.013 (release NotoSansArabic-v2.013), source github.com/notofonts/arabic, OFL-1.1, vendored as the FULL unmodified upstream Regular face at third_party/fonts/NotoSansArabic-Regular.ttf (sha256 bd86ca02f087d7f3c3788ba458fb6b73744c7639ed276b8d870dba6def6c40d0) beside its upstream OFL.txt; verified NO Reserved Font Name; the complex-script face a8's RTL/ligature shaping DoD needs. RUNTIME-RASTERIZE ONLY: the engine NEVER redistributes a pre-baked glyph atlas (a pre-baked atlas is a derived font artifact that would drag Modified-Version analysis into every shipped build) — atlases are built at runtime from these embedded faces and never shipped. The fonts remain under OFL-1.1, NOT the Context Engine EULA. Trust boundary (v1): embedded trusted fonts ONLY — no user-suppliable font asset kind (R-SEC-006 fuzz follow-up stays declared).
